title: Fix black background hiding content and ensure proper color visibility

requirements.summary: Fix black background issue throughout the entire app by replacing all black or near-black backgrounds with appropriate light or themed backgrounds
acceptanceCriteria: No page in the app has a solid black (#000 or near-black) background that obscures content
All text has sufficient contrast against its background (WCAG AA minimum)
Landing page, dashboard, create wizard, invitation editor, and guest invitation page are all visually legible
Header, navigation, cards, forms, and buttons are all fully visible without requiring user action
The app uses the existing design system color tokens (ivory, crimson, gold, rose, charcoal) consistently

file_operations.description: Ensure the root app wrapper and router outlet do not apply black or dark backgrounds. Verify that no dark theme classes or inline styles are applied to the main app container.

Replace any black or near-black background colors with light ivory, cream, or gradient backgrounds using the design system tokens. Ensure all hero sections, feature sections, and CTAs have proper light backgrounds with sufficient text contrast.

Replace any black or dark background colors with ivory or light theme backgrounds. Ensure all invitation cards, headers, and action buttons are fully visible against light backgrounds with proper contrast.
Replace any black backgrounds with light ivory or cream backgrounds. Ensure the wizard container, step indicators, and all form sections use light theme colors from the design system.
Replace any black backgrounds with light theme colors. Ensure the editor tabs, form sections, and preview areas all use ivory or cream backgrounds with proper text contrast.

Replace any black backgrounds with template-appropriate light backgrounds (ivory, cream, or template-defined colors). Ensure the hero section, event timeline, photo gallery, RSVP form, and venue sections all have visible backgrounds with proper contrast.
Replace any black or dark header backgrounds with white, ivory, or light theme colors. Ensure navigation links and buttons have sufficient contrast and are fully visible.
Replace any black backgrounds with template-driven light backgrounds or gradients. Ensure couple names, photos, and decorative elements are fully visible with proper contrast.
Replace any black backgrounds in event cards with light or template-themed colors. Ensure all event details (title, date, time, venue) are visible with proper contrast against their backgrounds.
Replace any black backgrounds with light or template-themed colors. Ensure venue name, address, and directions are fully visible with sufficient contrast.
Replace any black form backgrounds with ivory or light theme colors. Ensure all form fields, labels, and buttons are fully visible with proper contrast.
Replace any black default backgrounds with ivory or template-defined light colors. Ensure the preview renders with visible backgrounds for all template variations.
Replace any black backgrounds with ivory or cream. Ensure all form fields and photo upload areas are fully visible.
Replace any black backgrounds with light theme colors. Ensure event cards and forms are fully visible with proper contrast.
Replace any black backgrounds with ivory or light colors. Ensure photo gallery and music uploader sections are fully visible.
Replace any black backgrounds with light theme colors. Ensure all tabs, selectors, and preview areas have visible backgrounds with proper contrast.
Replace any black backgrounds with ivory or cream. Ensure the review summary and all sections are fully legible.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix CSS custom properties and Tailwind color token definitions to ensure default light theme does not apply dark/black background values
acceptanceCriteria: The CSS --background variable resolves to a light color (e.g., ivory or white) in the default theme
The body element does not have a black or near-black background in the default state
Dark mode variables do not bleed into the light/default theme
All pages render with a visible background immediately on load without any flash of black

file_operations.description: Audit all CSS custom property definitions in the :root selector. Ensure --background is set to a light color value (ivory, white, or cream OKLCH) in the default theme. Verify --foreground is set to dark text color. Ensure dark mode variables under .dark or @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) do not affect the default light theme. Set body background to use var(--background) or an explicit light color. Remove any black (#000 or near-black) values from default theme variables.

Audit CSS custom properties and Tailwind directives in this global entry point. Ensure no black or near-black background colors are defined as defaults. Verify that any background or foreground color variables resolve to light theme values in the default (non-dark-mode) state.

Review the extended color palette and darkMode configuration. Ensure that the default background colors (using CSS variables or direct color definitions) resolve to light ivory, cream, or white values. Verify that dark mode tokens do not leak into the default light theme.
